policy:
# Agent Operational Policy

## 1. Information Gathering Rules

- IF conversation starts → THEN ask for first name, last name, and zip code before any other action
- IF `find_user_id_by_name_zip` returns error → THEN ask for email and call `find_user_id_by_email`
- IF user cannot provide zip (or declines) → THEN ask for email directly
- IF user provides email proactively without zip being asked → THEN use email directly
- IF user provides both zip and email when asked → THEN use email directly
- IF first email returns error → THEN ask user for another email and try immediately
- IF user_id found → THEN call `get_user_details` to retrieve account info
- IF user references orders without specifying order_id → THEN call `get_order_details` for all orders in the account
- IF user provides an order number → THEN attempt `get_order_details` with that number first; if error, ask user to verify
- IF user provides order number without "#W" prefix → THEN prepend "#W" when calling tool
- IF multiple items of the same product type exist in an order → THEN ask user to clarify which specific item they want actioned
- IF user request requires knowing all orders (e.g., address change across all pending, finding specific item type, bulk returns/cancels) → THEN retrieve all orders before acting
- IF user requests address change to another of their own orders' address → THEN retrieve orders to find the referenced address before modifying
- IF user asks for tracking number → THEN provide tracking_id from fulfillments if present
- IF user requests default address update to an address from their orders → THEN retrieve orders to find that address, then call `modify_user_address` without re-asking user to provide it
- IF user requests exchange/modification and product comparison needed → THEN call `get_product_details`
- IF product_id already known from order details → THEN call `get_product_details` directly (Observed: agent may still call `list_all_product_types` first)
- IF user asks how many options are available → THEN call `get_product_details` and count only variants with `available: true`

## 2. Selection Rules

- IF user requests cheapest variant → THEN filter only `available: true` variants and select minimum price
- IF user requests maximum/priciest of any spec → THEN filter `available: true` variants, select highest price variant
- IF user requests specific combination (color, size, material, style, ventilation, etc.) → THEN find exact match among `available: true` variants
- IF target variant is unavailable (`available: false`) → THEN do not select it; inform user and offer alternatives or cancellation
- IF multiple orders contain the target item type → THEN check all orders to find delivered/pending ones as appropriate
- IF user requests "bigger size" → THEN offer available variants with larger capacity/size
- IF user requests color change on pending order → THEN find available variant with same product type and new color; maintain other options if possible
- IF user requests cheapest laptop with i7 or better → THEN filter `available: true` variants with i7 or i9, select minimum price
- IF user requests specific size/material combination → THEN find exact match among `available: true` variants; if no exact match, inform user
- IF user requests item with specific processor/RAM/storage combination → THEN find exact match among `available: true` variants; if unavailable, present closest and ask confirmation
- IF user requests "same type as already received" item → THEN identify the variant in the delivered order and use that item_id as the new_item_id for modification
- IF user requests exchange for same item (identical variant) → THEN use same item_id as both old and new; process as exchange
- IF user requests "priciest/most expensive" version with constraint → THEN filter `available: true` variants matching constraint, select highest price
- IF user specifies "more expensive" of two items → THEN compare prices and identify higher-priced item (Observed: conv 3, agent identified $989.70 tablet as pricier than $938.92)
- IF cheapest variant for a given item is already the current item → THEN keep that item unchanged in modification call

## 3. Action Execution Rules

- IF return requested on delivered order → THEN call `return_delivered_order_items` with correct item_ids and payment_method_id
- IF exchange requested on delivered order → THEN call `exchange_delivered_order_items` with item_ids, new_item_ids, and payment_method_id
- IF modification requested on pending order → THEN call `modify_pending_order_items` with item_ids, new_item_ids, and payment_method_id
- IF address change requested for pending order → THEN call `modify_pending_order_address`
- IF user asks to update all pending orders' addresses → THEN call `modify_pending_order_address` once per pending order sequentially
- IF user requests both address change on all pending orders AND default address update → THEN call `modify_pending_order_address` for each pending order first, then call `modify_user_address` once
- IF refund/charge amount differs from original → THEN calculate and state the difference to user before proceeding
- IF first tool call fails with error → THEN retry with correct parameters immediately without asking user
- IF cancel pending orders requested → THEN ask user for the reason before cancelling; use the stated reason
- IF user confirms cancellation without providing reason → THEN use default reason "no longer needed"
- IF user provides cancellation reason unprompted → THEN use that reason without asking again; do not override with default
- IF return requested on cancelled order → THEN do not attempt return (already refunded)
- IF user requests return of items from multiple different delivered orders → THEN call `return_delivered_order_items` separately for each order
- IF user wants to return items from same delivered order together → THEN include all item_ids in single `return_delivered_order_items` call
- IF cancellation requested but user only wants to remove one item → THEN inform user only full order cancellation is possible for pending orders
- IF refund payment method requested is not the original payment method → THEN inform user refund can only go to original payment method; offer transfer to human agent if user insists
- IF original payment was gift card and user requests refund to credit card → THEN refuse; inform user refund can only go back to gift card
- IF user accepts gift card refund after being told credit card is not possible → THEN process return with gift card as payment_method_id
- IF user requests return of all items in an order (after abandoning original specific return request) → THEN include all item_ids in single `return_delivered_order_items` call
- IF user requests modification not possible → THEN inform user and offer alternatives
- IF order is pending and user wants a "return" → THEN offer cancellation instead
- IF modifying multiple pending orders with same new item → THEN call `modify_pending_order_items` separately for each order
- IF two item modifications requested for same pending order → THEN include both in a single `modify_pending_order_items` call
- IF user requests exchanges across multiple items in same delivered order → THEN call `exchange_delivered_order_items` once with all item_ids and new_item_ids
- IF account address already matches desired address → THEN skip `modify_user_address` and inform user
- IF multiple pending orders exist and user requests cancellation of all → THEN cancel each separately with one `cancel_pending_order` call per order
- IF desired variant for modification is unavailable → THEN inform user and offer to cancel entire order
- IF user requests address change to "default" address → THEN use address from `get_user_details` profile without asking user to re-provide

## 4. Confirmation Rules

- IF price difference exists between old and new item → THEN state exact price difference and wait for confirmation before executing
- IF new item is cheaper than current → THEN state refund amount and payment method; proceed upon user confirmation
- IF address change requested across multiple orders and default → THEN confirm scope with user before executing (Observed: conv 1, user confirmed "all pending + default" before agent executed)
- IF payment method for refund/charge is ambiguous → THEN ask user which payment method to use before executing
- IF user says "same card" or "original payment" → THEN use payment_method_id from order's payment_history without further confirmation
- IF user confirms "yes, proceed" clearly → THEN execute without requesting additional confirmation
- IF modifying multiple orders → THEN ask user to confirm before executing all modifications (ask once, not per order)
- IF user confirms which specific item to return after clarification → THEN proceed without further confirmation
- IF user states payment preference clearly → THEN agent may proceed without additional explicit confirmation
- IF exchange involves two items and one payment method → THEN confirm total price difference across both items before executing single combined call
- IF user changes mind mid-confirmation → THEN accept updated preference and proceed without re-confirming original
- IF agent identifies non-target items to return (based on user's category filter) → THEN present list to user for confirmation before processing return
- IF user requests bulk cancellations and returns in single request → THEN confirm plan with user before executing all; execute sequentially once confirmed
- IF user requests partial order cancellation and agent informs only full cancellation is possible → THEN wait for user decision before proceeding; do not auto-cancel
- IF agent presents a single available option for exchange → THEN present it to user and wait for confirmation before executing
- IF user requests cancellation after being told desired modification is unavailable → THEN ask for reason before proceeding
- IF user requests return of multiple items and payment method is unambiguous (single payment method per order) → THEN proceed with that payment method without asking
- IF user is asking informational questions about return possibility (not requesting action) → THEN answer the question without processing any action; wait for explicit return request
- IF user asks for confirmation that address was changed → THEN confirm the new address details without re-executing the tool
- IF user explicitly states condition for swap (e.g., "only if cheaper") → THEN check condition first; if not met, inform user and skip action

## 5. Tool Usage Constraints

- IF `find_user_id_by_name_zip` succeeds → THEN call `get_user_details` immediately after
- IF `get_user_details` returns orders list → THEN retrieve order details as needed; do not act on orders without retrieving details first
- IF product_id is known → THEN call `get_product_details` directly with that product_id
- IF `list_all_product_types` is called → THEN use the returned product_id to call `get_product_details` (Observed: agent called `list_all_product_types` even when product_id was already known from order)
- IF `modify_pending_order_address` is called for multiple orders → THEN call sequentially, one per order
- IF `modify_user_address` is needed alongside order address changes → THEN call after all order address modifications are complete
- IF exchange tool returns success → THEN inform user and proceed to next requested action without waiting for additional confirmation

## 6. State-Dependent Rules

- IF order status is "pending" → THEN eligible for `modify_pending_order_items`, `modify_pending_order_address`, `cancel_pending_order`
- IF order status is "delivered" → THEN eligible for `return_delivered_order_items`, `exchange_delivered_order_items`
- IF order status is "processed" or "exchange requested" or "return requested" → THEN not eligible for modification/cancellation; inform user
- IF order status is "cancelled" → THEN not eligible for return; inform user it was already refunded
- IF user requests return of item originally paid by gift card → THEN refund must go to gift card; cannot redirect to credit card
- IF user requests return of item originally paid by credit card → THEN refund goes to credit card; cannot redirect to gift card
- IF multiple payment methods exist on account → THEN refund goes to the payment_method_id recorded in the specific order's payment_history
- IF user insists on refund to non-original payment method → THEN inform user it is not possible and offer transfer to human agent
- IF user abandons request for non-original payment method refund and accepts original payment method → THEN proceed with return using original payment method
- IF user requests to return all items from an order after originally requesting partial return → THEN include all item_ids in the return call (Observed: conv 3)
- IF address found in one order matches what user references → THEN use that address for modifications without asking user to re-type it
- IF user requests Washington DC address (or any specific city) across all pending orders → THEN retrieve all orders, identify pending ones, apply address change to each pending order, then update default address
